recommended_cleanup_method: 301 redirect plus remove or hide from discovery; use canonical override only as a temporary fallback
decision_summary: Merge the TikTok username intent into /tools/username-generator/ and stop generating a standalone discoverable TikTok username page. Keep noindex constraints untouched during cleanup; do not index the TikTok slug.
username_generator_exists_and_indexable: Yes. src/data/tools.ts defines username-generator and the current dist page renders robots index, follow plus googlebot index, follow. The existing copy already mentions TikTok and carries tiktok username generator as a secondary keyword, so it can absorb this intent with safer copy/FAQ refinements.
tiktok_current_discovery_presence: Present because src/data/tools.ts contains a tiktok-username-generator entry. That entry feeds getStaticPaths in src/pages/tools/[slug].astro, the all-tools discovery grid in src/pages/tools/index.astro, category pages via getToolsByCategory in src/pages/categories/[slug].astro, the HTML sitemap in src/pages/sitemap/index.astro, and the Astro sitemap integration after build.
current_internal_links_relatedslugs: No other source relatedSlugs were found pointing to tiktok-username-generator. The TikTok page itself points to youtube-name-generator and username-generator. Future internal links should point to username-generator, not tiktok-username-generator.
current_sitemap_presence: Present in current generated output because the data entry generates /tools/tiktok-username-generator/ and sitemap outputs include generated tool routes. Removing or hiding the entry after a redirect should remove it from tools index, category page, HTML sitemap, and XML sitemap on rebuild.
current_route_redirect_support: The dynamic tool route currently does not implement slug-specific redirects. getStaticPaths returns only tools.map(...) and the page self-renders each tool. Category pages use Astro.redirect for missing categories, but the tool page does not have merge redirect logic.
current_canonical_override_support: Possible only with source work. BaseLayout accepts a canonical prop, and src/pages/tools/[slug].astro currently passes canonical={`/tools/${tool.slug}/`}. A per-slug canonical map could point tiktok-username-generator to /tools/username-generator/, but that would leave a duplicate noindex page live and is weaker than a redirect for cleanup.
removing_or_hiding_entry_impact: Removing the src/data/tools.ts entry without a redirect would stop generating the page and could create a 404 for existing links. With a 301 redirect in config/routing, removal is safe and also cleans discovery, category, HTML sitemap, and XML sitemap surfaces. If a hidden-tool mechanism is preferred, it would need to exclude hidden tools from discovery and sitemaps while either still rendering or redirecting the route.
redirect_feasibility: Feasible on Vercel/Astro, but not currently configured. Add a permanent redirect from /tools/tiktok-username-generator/ to /tools/username-generator/ in astro.config.mjs if the adapter/site config supports redirects, or add a scoped vercel.json redirect. Test both trailing and non-trailing slash behavior because trailingSlash is set to always.
exact_source_files_to_change_if_approved: src/data/tools.ts: strengthen username-generator copy, FAQ, and options to absorb TikTok/social handle brainstorming safely; remove or hide the tiktok-username-generator entry; optionally adjust tiktok-name-generator secondaryKeywords to reduce cannibalization. src/pages/tools/[slug].astro: remove tiktok-username-generator from pass20ControlMap/pass29SocialControls and Pass 29 social generation branch when entry is removed; remove the stale duplicate switch case grouped with roblox-username-generator; add a canonical override only if choosing the temporary canonical path. astro.config.mjs or vercel.json/routing layer: add a 301 redirect from /tools/tiktok-username-generator/ to /tools/username-generator/. Generated dist outputs are rebuilt, not edited manually.
implementation_sequence: 1. Update username-generator content so TikTok handle brainstorming is clearly covered with no availability, no affiliation, no impersonation, no growth/reach guarantee, and platform-rules review language. 2. Add the permanent redirect for /tools/tiktok-username-generator/ to /tools/username-generator/. 3. Remove or hide the tiktok-username-generator data entry so discovery, category, and sitemap surfaces stop linking it. 4. Clean stale tool-page generation references for tiktok-username-generator, including pass29 controls/branch and the duplicate legacy switch case. 5. Rebuild and scan source plus dist outputs. 6. Leave noindexToolSlugs unchanged unless a separate approval explicitly permits noindex list cleanup.
risk_assessment: Lowest risk approved path is redirect plus discovery removal. It preserves route equity and user bookmarks while removing doorway/thin duplicate surfaces. Canonical-only is lower engineering risk but higher SEO risk because the duplicate page remains discoverable/generated and noindex plus canonical sends mixed cleanup signals. Deleting the data entry without a redirect is not recommended because it risks 404s and stale external/internal references. Redirect config must be validated on the deployed adapter because the static dist folder alone may not show runtime redirect behavior.
validation_checklist: Run: npx.cmd tsc --noEmit; npx.cmd astro check; npm.cmd run build. Source scans: rg -n "tiktok-username-generator" src public astro.config.mjs vercel.json; rg -n "tiktok username generator|tiktok handle generator" src/data/tools.ts src/pages/tools/[slug].astro. Built-output scans: rg -n "/tools/tiktok-username-generator/|tiktok-username-generator|TikTok Username Generator" dist. Confirm dist/tools/index.html, dist/categories/social-media-tools/index.html, dist/sitemap/index.html, and dist/sitemap-0.xml do not list the TikTok slug. Confirm dist/tools/username-generator/index.html still exists and renders canonical /tools/username-generator/ with robots/googlebot index, follow. Redirect validation: inspect Vercel output or preview response headers and verify /tools/tiktok-username-generator/ returns a permanent redirect to /tools/username-generator/; also test the non-trailing slash form.
expected_post_implementation_state: /tools/tiktok-username-generator/ permanently redirects to /tools/username-generator/. The TikTok slug is absent from generated discovery/navigation, category listings, HTML sitemap, and XML sitemap. username-generator remains the destination for username intent and keeps safe platform-neutral framing. noindexToolSlugs remains unchanged during this cleanup.
notes: Created as an implementation plan only. No source files changed, no indexing changes made, and no commit made.
